bone.$description: Bone — Page canvas — warm off-white that makes the black ink read like printed type rather than screen pixels

carbon.$description: Carbon — Primary text, body copy, headlines, section rules — the single ink that carries the whole page

ash.$description: Ash — Muted helper text, timestamps, secondary metadata — desaturated enough to recede behind Carbon

lampblack.$description: Lampblack — Hard borders on outlined controls, maximum-emphasis rules — used sparingly where Carbon is not black enough

itcgaramondstdlightnarrow.$description: All statement and display headlines. The Light Narrow cut is the signature — its condensed proportions let massive sizes (up to 130px) fit on a line while the 0.90 line-height stacks lines into a dense editorial block. Substituting Cormorant Garamond Light or EB Garamond with a tight tracking and line-height override will read close enough.

ppneuemontreal.$description: Everything non-headline: body text, section labels, timestamps, nav, column headings, footer. Weight 500 for body, 600 for small bold labels (section titles, column headings). The 0.01em tracking opens the grotesque just enough to feel print-set rather than digital. Substituting Inter or Neue Haas Grotesk at the same weight/track will read close enough.

bone.$description: Surface level 1: The only surface — the entire page is one flat warm-white field with no elevated tiers
